brief.pull_quote: 40% of new SaaS MVPs in 2026 shipped via vibe coding. Almost none passed a security review before launch.
brief.angle: Pick by job, not by hype — 10 tools, 4 tiers, and the security caveat nobody's flagging.
brief.title_draft: The 10-tool vibe-coding stack 92% of devs use — and the one caveat nobody is reading

slides.copy: 41% of code in 2026 is AI-written. Most of it ships without a security review. Here is the 10-tool stack — and the caveat nobody flags.

Style: cinematic editorial lighting, muted warm-to-cool palette, light film grain, subtle vignette. No watermark, no extra labels.

slides.direct_answer_block: The 10 best vibe-coding tools for 2026, grouped by job: Cursor and GitHub Copilot for daily productivity, Claude Code and Windsurf for multi-file refactors, Replit Agent, Bolt.new, and Lovable for fast prototypes, V0, Aider, and Amazon Q Developer for specialized frontend, CLI, and enterprise work. Skip any tool that does not fit your actual job — and review AI-generated code for auth and data-handling flaws before shipping.

slides.copy: Which tier broke first for you — productivity core, multi-file brains, ship-fast lane, or specialists? Tell me the tool and the moment it failed. The blog breakdown with scorecards is in the first comment.
